pattern: Binary search over the answer space: the minimum viable speed is the smallest integer k for which the total hours to finish all piles does not exceed the limit.
hints: The answer lies somewhere between 1 and max(piles); the feasibility function is monotone — once a speed works, any higher speed also works.
To check whether speed k is feasible, sum ceil(pile / k) for each pile and compare with h.
Binary-search on k: keep a range [lo, hi] and test the midpoint each iteration, tightening toward the smallest feasible value.

edge_inputs.description: four piles with more hours than piles, classic example
speed forced to max pile because h equals number of piles
one extra hour allows slightly slower speed
single pile, h equals one — must eat everything at once
single pile, many hours — answer is 1
all piles equal one and h equals pile count — any speed >= 1 works so answer is 1
h exactly equals piles.length so no slack — must finish each pile in one hour

stress.description: large random pile array with h set to a small multiple of pile count
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    piles = [rng.randint(1, 10**9) for _ in range(n)]
    h = rng.randint(n, min(n * 10, 10**9))
    return [piles, h]
stress.note: h >= n ensures a solution always exists
